name: Chocolate Mousse Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the chocolate and water over very low heat, stirring constantly until smooth.
2. Remove from heat and stir in the beaten egg yolks.
3. Add vanilla and beat.
4. In a large mixing bowl, beat egg whites until foamy.
5. Gradually add sugar to the egg whites, beating until stiff peaks form.
6. Add a small amount of the egg white mixture to the chocolate, then fold in the remaining.
7. Pour into a greased 9-inch pie pan, which has been sprinkled with sugar.
8. Refrigerate for the rest of the mousse.
9. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 25 minutes, until set.
10. Chill the crust for 1 hour.
11. The center will fall to form a chocolate meringue shell.
12. Spoon the chilled mousse into the shell and refrigerate overnight.
This is our family's all-time favorite Pesach dessert, for the Seder or any time. Enjoy it year-round!
